nodes.title: Pre-Development Foundation
nodes.description: Documentation, standards, infrastructure before production code.
nodes.prompt:
CONTEXT: Pre-Development Foundation — Wave 1

You are setting up the mgit project scaffold. mgit is a safety-critical micro version control system for LLM coding agents, built in Go. It uses go-git v5 (pure Go, no shell-out to git binary) and modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, CGO-free) as its two core storage engines.

ARCHITECTURE: mgit follows a strict layered architecture:
  cmd/mgit/ → internal/service/ → internal/store/ → internal/model/
  Dependency direction is downward only. No circular imports.

KEY STANDARDS:
  - DO-178C (avionics), IEC 62304 (medical devices), NASA-STD-8739.8
  - 90%+ line coverage overall, 95%+ for store/service layers
  - Zero linter warnings, zero race detector issues
  - TDD mandatory: write test first (red), implement (green), refactor

APPROVED PACKAGES: go-git/go-git/v5, modernc.org/sqlite, spf13/cobra, labstack/echo/v4, oklog/ulid/v2, stretchr/testify, golang.org/x/sync, log/slog (stdlib). No others without approval per PACKAGE-APPROVAL-PROCESS.md.

READ BEFORE CODING: CLAUDE.md (directives), CODING-STYLE.md (patterns), QUALITY-STANDARDS.md (coverage), TDD-WORKFLOW.md (process), APPROVED-PACKAGES.md (deps).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Quality & Process Documentation
nodes.description: Create all required documentation standards and process guides.
nodes.prompt:
SCOPE: Create all 6 governing documentation files that establish quality standards, coding conventions, testing process, and dependency management for mgit development. These documents form the contract that all LLM coding agents must follow.

FILES TO CREATE: QUALITY-STANDARDS.md (coverage targets, static analysis, security), CODING-STYLE.md (Go conventions, architecture patterns, SQL rules), CONTRIBUTING-LLM.md (agent guidelines, forbidden patterns), TDD-WORKFLOW.md (test-driven process, test categories), APPROVED-PACKAGES.md (audited dependency list), PACKAGE-APPROVAL-PROCESS.md (new dependency evaluation).

All documents must reference DO-178C, IEC 62304, and NASA-STD-8739.8 standards where applicable.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Project Scaffolding
nodes.description: Initialize Go module, directory structure, build tools, and test infrastructure.
nodes.prompt:
SCOPE: Initialize the Go project with all approved dependencies, create the directory structure per CODING-STYLE.md, set up build tooling (Makefile), configure linting (.golangci.yml), and create test infrastructure utilities.

DIRECTORY STRUCTURE: cmd/mgit/, internal/model/, internal/store/git/, internal/store/index/, internal/service/, internal/api/http/, internal/mcp/, internal/mtix/, internal/testutil/, e2e/.

TEST INFRASTRUCTURE: MakeTestCommit, MakeTestBranch, MakeTestDiff fixtures; NewTestStore (temp SQLite); MockClock (deterministic time); NewTestGitRepo (temp go-git repository). All helpers must support cleanup/defer pattern.

WAVE GATE: make build succeeds, make lint passes, test helpers compile.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Initialize Go module with approved dependencies
nodes.prompt:
Run go mod init github.com/astutic/mgit and configure go.mod for Go 1.23+ minimum version. Add all approved dependencies from APPROVED-PACKAGES.md: github.com/go-git/go-git/v5 (v5.11.0+), modernc.org/sqlite (v1.28.0+), github.com/spf13/cobra (v1.8.0+), github.com/labstack/echo/v4 (v4.12.0+), github.com/stretchr/testify (v1.9.0+), github.com/oklog/ulid/v2 (v2.1.0+), golang.org/x/sync (latest), golang.org/x/crypto (latest). Execute go mod tidy to resolve all transitive dependencies. Verify that there are no conflicts with security policies per NFR-5.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
go.mod file exists in repository root with module path github.com/astutic/mgit, go directive set to 1.23, all approved dependencies listed with correct versions, go mod tidy executes without errors, go list -m all shows clean dependency tree.

REQUIRED TESTS:
  - TestGoMod_Exists
  - TestGoMod_CorrectModulePath
  - TestGoMod_GoVersionDirective
  - TestGoMod_AllApprovedDepsPresent
  - TestGoMod_NoUnauthorizedDeps
  - TestGoMod_TidySucceeds
nodes.acceptance: go.mod file exists in repository root with module path github.com/astutic/mgit, go directive set to 1.23, all approved dependencies listed with correct versions, go mod tidy executes without errors, go list -m all shows clean dependency tree.

Each target must be phony and have a help comment. Set DEFAULT_GOAL to test to encourage testing before building.

nodes.title: Concurrent CLI race conditions: file lock + audit
nodes.prompt: 10-agent stress test reproduced two race conditions when CLI processes operate on the same repo concurrently: (1) SQLite PRAGMA WAL contention 'database is locked (5)', (2) go-git HEAD 'reference not found'. Both produce 3-7% failure rates. Need process-level file lock + concurrency audit before beta release.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Implement process-level flock for CLI mutual exclusion
nodes.prompt: Add internal/store/lock package with FileLock that uses flock(2) on .mgit/locks/mgit.lock. Acquire in OpenApp before any store opens. Release on App.Close. 30s default timeout. Stress test with 10 concurrent CLI processes must achieve 100% success.
nodes.acceptance: 10 concurrent agents x 3 commits each = 30/30 success across 5 consecutive runs. Lock timeout returns ErrLockHeld with holder PID.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Security and safety-critical audit of file lock implementation
nodes.prompt: Audit the new file lock against CLAUDE.md security mindset, OWASP ASVS L2, DO-178C/IEC 62304 principles. Specific concerns: TOCTOU on lockfile, stale lock from killed processes, symlink attacks on lockfile path, file descriptor leaks on partial failures, signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM) and lock release, Windows compatibility (flock syscall is Unix-only).
nodes.acceptance: All audit items addressed or documented as acceptable risk with rationale.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Concurrency stress test suite for CI
nodes.prompt: Add e2e/concurrent_cli_test.go with: 10 agents same repo different tasks (was failing, must pass), 10 agents separate repos, 5 agents same task race. Tests must run in CI as regression guards.
nodes.acceptance: All 3 tests pass on race detector. No flakiness across 10 consecutive runs.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Sandboxed Agent Execution (microVM)
nodes.description: Seamless, platform-agnostic microVM sandbox bound to mgit task-worktrees so LLM agents execute untrusted code (builds, installs, tests) with no host exposure and full per-commit provenance. Implements FR-17 per ADR-005 and both audits.
nodes.prompt: STORY CONTEXT: Build the mgit sandbox (FR-17) — one task <-> one worktree <-> one microVM. The guest is the hostile party (it runs the npm/supply-chain code); the host is the trust anchor. Read FIRST: docs/adr/005-microvm-sandbox.md, AUDIT-FR17-SANDBOX-V1.md (F-01..F-12), AUDIT-FR17-SANDBOX-SECURITY-V1.md (SEC-01..SEC-12), plus CLAUDE.md governing rules. Dependency order (do not violate): 11.1 requirements -> 11.2 model -> 11.3 store -> 11.4 daemon -> 11.5 backends -> 11.6 guest-fs -> 11.7 network -> 11.8 attestation/land -> 11.9 service -> 11.10 cli -> 11.11 agent-integration -> 11.12 verification/compliance -> 11.13 e2e/perf/fuzz. Architecture: cmd -> service -> store/manager -> model. Handlers never touch store/manager directly. DI everywhere, clock injected, no globals/init(). CGO confined to mgit-sandboxd; core stays pure-Go. STORY-CLOSURE GATE (mandatory before the story is marked done): an INDEPENDENT, detailed code review of the entire story's delivered code must be performed by a reviewer that did NOT write it (separate agent/session or human). The review covers: requirement traceability (every FR-17/NFR-17 acceptance criterion -> code -> test), the layered-architecture & dependency rules, SQL/append-only laws, go-git rules, the security-audit remediations (SEC-01..SEC-12, F-01..F-12), function/file size limits, and anti-stub verification. Findings are logged and resolved before closure. Use the security-review skill for the security pass. The reviewer must be independent of the implementer. COMMIT MESSAGE: follow CLAUDE.md format {type}({scope}): {desc} + 'Refs: MGIT-11.x'. Do NOT add a 'Co-authored-by' trailer, an agent name, an assistant signature, or any 'Generated with' line. Rationale: commit metadata is an audit artifact and must not disclose the internal agent/tooling used to produce the change (information-exposure control).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Draft FR-17 / NFR-17 in REQUIREMENTS.md

Capture every behavior in ADR-005: task<->worktree<->microVM binding, lazy provision, transparent exec routing, quarantine-then-land, per-VM network policy (none/allowlist/open), host-anchored attestation, capability escalation, zero-touch lifecycle. Each criterion gets a stable FR-17.x / NFR-17.x id for traceability. Cross-reference ADR-002 (dual hash), ADR-004 (worktree sharing tension, SEC-03).

nodes.title: CLI/daemon wiring for image registration (images.Register)
nodes.prompt:
MGIT-11.13.4 added the host-side image signer/registrar (internal/sandboxd/images: BuildEntry/Sign/Register/LoadSigningKey) — the library that computes digests, Ed25519-signs, and writes images.lock. It has no CLI/daemon caller yet; today only tests + the e2e invoke it. Wire a host operator surface to register a built guest image into images.lock, e.g. `mgit sandbox image add --name <n> --kernel <vmlinux> --rootfs <rootfs> --cmdline <args>` (computes digests via BuildEntry, signs with the trust-root key via LoadSigningKey, writes via Register, prints the digest-pinned ref). Also consider `mgit sandbox image ls` / trust-root init surfacing GenerateTrustRoot. Pairs with scripts/build-guest-image.sh (which produces the rootfs this registers).

DEFINITION OF DONE per issue: TDD; REQUIRED TESTS pass; coverage gates; zero lint/vet/race/vuln; /simplify after green; handlers go through the images library (no duplicated signing logic). Commit format + no co-authored-by/agent/Generated-with trailer.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
A host operator can register a built guest image (kernel+rootfs+cmdline) into images.lock from the CLI; it is signed against the trust root and immediately resolvable by the sandbox (Store.Resolve); the command prints the digest-pinned image reference.

REQUIRED TESTS:
  - TestImageAdd_RegistersAndResolves
  - TestImageAdd_MissingTrustRoot_FailsClosed
nodes.acceptance: A host operator can register a built guest image into images.lock from the CLI; it is signed against the trust root and immediately resolvable (Store.Resolve); the command prints the digest-pinned reference.

nodes.title: Harden ADR-002 content_hash (omitted fields + sub-second time)
nodes.description: The ADR-002 content_hash formula (SHA-256 over message+file_diffs+task_id+parent+created_at) omits AgentID/CreatedBy/SessionID/CommitType/Branch and truncates created_at to second precision. Two distinct audit records can therefore collide on content_hash. Found by the MGIT-11.8 closure security pass (F3); pre-existing, not introduced by 11.8.
nodes.prompt:
The authoritative ADR-002 content_hash is model.Commit.ComputeContentHash = SHA-256(message + file_diffs_json + task_id + parent_id + created_at), with created_at formatted to SECOND precision ("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z"). The MGIT-11.8 closure security pass (F3, MEDIUM) found:
1. The hash OMITS AgentID, CreatedBy, SessionID, CommitType, Branch — two commits differing only in (e.g.) AgentID produce the SAME content_hash, so the "authoritative compliance identifier" does not uniquely identify the audited record, weakening "if hashes diverge, the commit was tampered with".
2. created_at is truncated to second precision — sub-second differences collide.
3. The formula uses parent_id (parent SHA-1) where ADR-002's prose says parent_content_hash.

This is a PRE-EXISTING design issue (predates FR-17; 11.8 only extracted the function to model and single-sourced it). It was NOT changed in 11.8 because changing content_hash REWRITES every existing commit's hash — a breaking, migration-bearing change to ADR-002 that needs its own ADR revision + data-migration plan.

SCOPE: revise ADR-002 to define a complete, collision-resistant content_hash (include the identity-bearing fields; full-precision UTC time; resolve the parent_id-vs-parent_content_hash wording), with a migration/versioning strategy (e.g. a content_hash version tag) so existing task_commits remain verifiable. Coordinate with FR-3.1, FR-12.3, and the sandbox land re-verification (FR-17.24) which recomputes via the same function. Owner decision: whether to version the hash or accept the documented limitation for v1.

Refs: ADR-002, FR-3.1, FR-12.3, FR-17.24; found in MGIT-11.8 closure (F3).
nodes.acceptance: A decision (revise vs. accept-documented) recorded; if revised, ADR-002 updated with a complete content_hash definition + a migration/versioning plan that keeps existing commits verifiable, and model.Commit.ComputeContentHash updated accordingly with tests.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Linux guest image with mgit-guest as PID 1 (build, sign, register)

The fcvsock transport is already proven on real KVM (MGIT-13). No co-authored-by/agent/Generated-with trailer.

nodes.title: Core mgit Windows-host portability (Unix-first assumptions)

DECISION REQUIRED: is Windows a supported HOST platform for core mgit, or only the target for the sandbox guest backend? nodes.title: Firecracker worktree delivery (no virtiofs)
nodes.prompt: Firecracker has NO virtiofs (the vzf path is MGIT-11.6.1). May need an ADR-005 amendment recording per-backend worktree semantics. nodes.title: Guest writable root (overlay) for arbitrary identical-path worktree mounts
nodes.prompt:
DISCOVERED in MGIT-11.13.4 (real exec round-trip, 2026-06-20). The guest rootfs is mounted READ-ONLY (FR-17.17 immutable pinned image), so mgit-guest (PID 1) can only mount the worktree over a PRE-EXISTING mount point — it cannot os.MkdirAll an arbitrary host worktree path (e.g. /home/user/repo/worktrees/task) on a read-only root (the mkdir fails). The cached CI image sidesteps this with an external /sbin/overlay-init that makes / writable via overlayfs(lower=squashfs RO, upper=overlay COW drive vdb) then switch_roots. mgit-guest needs the equivalent so the identical-path worktree mount (FR-17.3, MGIT-11.6.5) works for ANY host path, not just a baked one.

SCOPE: make the guest root writable before mounting the worktree. Option A (preferred): mgit-guest, as PID 1, mounts an overlayfs (lowerdir = the RO image root, upperdir/workdir on the backend's overlay drive vdb or a tmpfs) and switch_root/pivot_root into it, then continues (mount /proc already done, /dev, worktree at its now-creatable identical path, /tmp). Option B: ship overlay-init in the guest image and chain init -> mgit-guest after it sets up the writable root. The backend already attaches the overlay drive (vdb); wiring mgit-guest to consume it as the upper is the natural fit. This also removes the os.MkdirAll-on-RO-root limitation noted in MGIT-11.6.5 and lets MGIT-11.13.4's e2e use a t.TempDir() worktree (drop the baked-mountpoint requirement, MGIT_E2E_WORKTREE).

DEFINITION OF DONE: TDD where unit-testable (the overlay/switch_root path is e2e-gated on KVM); a firecracker guest mounts a worktree at an ARBITRARY (not pre-baked) identical absolute path and an exec reads it (extend TestE2E_Exec_RealGuest_RoundTrip to a t.TempDir worktree); zero lint/vet/vuln; /simplify; no co-authored-by/agent/Generated-with trailer.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
A firecracker guest with a read-only image root mounts the worktree at an arbitrary identical absolute host path (created at runtime, not baked into the image); the guest root is writable for the mount-point creation without mutating the pinned image; the e2e round-trip works with a t.TempDir worktree.

REQUIRED TESTS:
  - TestGuestRoot_WritableForArbitraryWorktreePath (e2e/KVM)
  - TestE2E_Exec_RealGuest_RoundTrip updated to a runtime worktree path
nodes.acceptance: A firecracker guest with a read-only pinned image root mounts the worktree at an arbitrary identical absolute host path created at runtime (not baked into the image), without mutating the image; the e2e exec round-trip passes with a t.TempDir worktree.

nodes.title: vsock CID peer-binding (SEC-10)
nodes.prompt:
Bind each guest's vsock control/land/attestation channel to its VM context ID (CID): mgit-sandboxd verifies the source CID of every vsock connection matches the sandbox the channel was provisioned for, so one guest can never address another sandbox's land or attestation channel (SEC-10; ADR-005 adoption criterion 11). Specify CID authentication in the vsock protocol spec (extends MGIT-11.8.2); reject and audit mismatched CIDs.

DEFINITION OF DONE per issue: TDD red->green->refactor; all REQUIRED TESTS exist and pass; coverage gates met (Store/Service 95%/90%, other 90%/85%); zero linter/vet/race/vuln findings. REQUIRED TESTS:
  - TestVsock_CIDMismatch_Rejected
  - TestVsock_CrossSandboxChannel_Unreachable
  - TestVsock_CIDBindingAudited

nodes.title: Concrete land adapters (ObjectImporter/Brancher) + FileDiffs-to-tree binding
nodes.prompt: The land orchestrator (MGIT-11.9.3) injects ObjectImporter, CommitAppender, Brancher ports; CommitAppender is real (index.AppendTaskCommits) but ObjectImporter (write verified git objects into the host shared .mgit store) and Brancher (taskID->branch fast-forward; git.MergeStore.FastForward exists but takes a branchName) need concrete host-git-store adapters wired in the daemon/CLI. ALSO complete the SEC-06 binding gap: land.VerifyBinding binds the commit object's fields but NOT that the claimed FileDiffs match the landed tree objects (content_hash is self-consistent over the CLAIMED diffs). Recompute diffs from the landed tree objects and bind them; enforce land.ValidateTreePath (already present, currently unused) per tree entry. Found in the MGIT-11.9.3 closure. No co-authored-by/agent/Generated-with trailer.
nodes.acceptance: land imports verified objects into the host shared store and fast-forwards the task branch via concrete adapters; FileDiffs are recomputed from the landed tree objects and a mismatch fails land (ErrLandVerificationFailed); ValidateTreePath is enforced per tree entry.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: FR-17 exec/land minor hardening follow-ons
nodes.prompt: Collect minor deferred hardening from the 11.9 / MGIT-13 /simplify + closure passes: (a) SHARE the guest vsock-port constant (1024) between firecracker.guestVsockPort and cmd/mgit-guest's --vsock-port flag default — single source (e.g. model.DefaultGuestVsockPort). (b) guestexec.Run can block forever in ReadFrame against a hung/hostile guest when neither a ctx deadline nor req.Timeout is set — add a default host-side read deadline/floor (e.g. TTL-derived) so a stalled guest cannot hang exec indefinitely. (c) Land audit-after-persist retry contract: a post-persist EventLanded failure currently returns an error on an ALREADY-DURABLE land; make Land() retry-idempotent (AppendTaskCommits rejects dup task+position; import idempotent; fast-forward idempotent) OR demote the post-persist audit failure to a logged warning, and document/test the chosen contract. No co-authored-by/agent/Generated-with trailer.
nodes.acceptance: the guest vsock port is single-sourced across host dialer and guest; a hung guest cannot block exec indefinitely (a default deadline applies); the land post-persist audit-failure / retry contract is explicit and tested.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Wire PeerBinder.Bind/Invalidate into launch/teardown (SEC-10)
nodes.prompt: internal/sandboxd.PeerBinder exists (Bind/Invalidate/Authorize) but NO backend/lifecycle calls Bind at launch or Invalidate at teardown, so incoming guest->host land/attestation channels cannot be peer-authorized (SEC-10/FR-17.27). Wire the launch lifecycle to Bind(sandboxID, peerID) with the HOST-OBSERVED peer identity (the vsock CID, e.g. "cid:3" for firecracker, or VM-GUID), and Invalidate at teardown. Decide ownership: the daemon owns PeerBinder; the manager/backend knows the CID — surface it without leaking the seam. The CID is host-observed, never guest-asserted (SEC-05). No co-authored-by/agent/Generated-with trailer.
nodes.acceptance: Every launched sandbox is Bound to its host-observed peer identity; teardown Invalidates it; an incoming channel whose source peer differs from the binding is rejected (ErrPeerBindingMismatch); a recycled CID cannot inherit a destroyed sandbox's channel.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Windows-native sandbox backend (WCOW)
nodes.description: Native Windows sandbox backend using Hyper-V-isolated Windows containers (WCOW), Windows guest, host-matching. Deferred until there is developer demand for Windows-host support; Windows hosts are served by the audited container fallback until then. Implements ADR-006 / FR-17.39.
nodes.prompt:
EPIC: Windows-native sandbox backend (WCOW) for FR-17. Build the Windows sandbox backend per ADR-006 and FR-17.39: the guest is a WINDOWS guest in a Hyper-V-isolated Windows container (WCOW), NOT a Linux guest (LCOW). Host-matching principle — a Windows developer's toolchain (npm/python/go/cargo/MSVC) runs on Windows, so the sandbox isolates a Windows execution environment; this also removes Linux<->Windows path/EOL/permission translation at the land boundary.

WHY DEFERRED: MGIT-11.5.3 reached a decision + validation but no implementation. Pick this epic up when developers request Windows-host sandbox support. Until then Windows hosts use the reduced-isolation container fallback (--backend container --acknowledge-reduced-isolation).

VALIDATED (2026-06-15 WCOW pilot, recorded on MGIT-11.5.3): on a Win10 Pro 22H2 host, docker --isolation=hyperv booted an ltsc2019 Windows container, and Go 1.23.4 / Node 20.18.1+npm (React/Angular) / Python 3.12.7 / C (gcc 14.2) / C++ (g++ 14.2 STL) / Rust 1.96 (gnu) all compiled+ran inside it. So the guest environment is proven; this epic is the productionization.

KEY CONSTRAINTS (read ADR-006, ADR-005, FR-17.* esp. FR-17.39/27/35/15/16/17/31, AUDIT-FR17-SANDBOX-*):
- Drive WCOW HEADLESSLY via the HCS API (vmcompute.dll/computecore.dll via x/sys/windows) and/or containerd — NEVER Docker Desktop (GUI/session-bound, unfit for a daemon; it was the source of all SSH-pilot flakiness).
- Do NOT vendor hcsshim internal/ (unsupported-code supply-chain hazard); prefer the documented HCS public API or containerd's public client.
- Control/land channel = HvSocket (AF_HYPERV), peer-bound to the VM identity GUID (FR-17.27); framed protocol per FR-17.35; enforce ceilings.
- Windows mgit-guest agent (the current one is //go:build linux); transport-only, no signing key (SEC-01).
- Prepared, digest-pinned Windows base image (FR-17.17/31, SANDBOX-IMAGES register, Ed25519 signing FR-17.29/38). Version matrix: ltsc2019 for a Win10 host, ltsc2022 for Win11/Server 2022 (Hyper-V isolation needs host build >= container base build).
- This backend will NOT reuse the Linux/macOS microvm.Manager seam (kernel+rootfs); it is a distinct container+UVM model, closer to a Hyper-V-isolated variant of the container backend. Still behind model.SandboxManager (FR-17.15), wired via select.go.
- All CLAUDE.md rules apply: TDD, coverage gates, pre-import vuln checks + package approval for any new dep, /simplify after green, story-closure independent review + security pass. Commit format {type}({scope}): {desc} + Refs: MGIT-12.x; NO co-authored-by / agent name / Generated-with trailer (information-exposure control).
- DEV/TEST target: Windows 11 Pro or Windows Server 2022 (for ltsc2022 + headless containerd). Win10 22H2 works only with ltsc2019.
nodes.acceptance: A Windows host launches a per-task Hyper-V-isolated Windows container (WCOW) via mgit; the developer's Windows toolchain runs inside it; the worktree is mounted at the identical path; control/land works over HvSocket with VM-GUID peer binding; quarantine-then-land enforces dual-hash + task binding + attestation identically to the Linux backend; teardown leaves no host residue; backend selectable via select.go; coverage + lint + vuln gates met; story-closure independent review + security pass completed.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: WCOW launch spike & API-surface decision
nodes.description: Prototype headless launch of a Hyper-V-isolated Windows container from Go and decide the API surface (no Docker Desktop, no internal-code vendoring).
nodes.prompt:
Spike + decide HOW to launch/manage a Hyper-V-isolated Windows container (WCOW) from Go, HEADLESSLY. Evaluate: (a) HCS API direct — vmcompute.dll/computecore.dll via golang.org/x/sys/windows, constructing the HCS compute-system JSON document for a Windows container utility VM; (b) containerd public client + containerd-shim-runhcs-v1 with the hyperv isolation runtime/annotation; (c) minimal hcsshim public surface. Reject Docker Desktop (GUI/session-bound) and vendoring hcsshim internal/uvm (unsupported code). DELIVERABLE: a working proof on a Win11 Pro / Server 2022 box — launch an isolated Windows container, exec a command, reach it over HvSocket, tear down with no host residue — plus a written recommendation of the API surface with tradeoffs. Pre-import vuln check + PACKAGE-APPROVAL for any new dependency before importing. Refs: ADR-006, FR-17.39, FR-17.15.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Documented decision (HCS-direct vs containerd vs hcsshim) with rationale, no Docker Desktop, no internal-code vendoring.
- A reproducible proof script that launches an isolated Windows container headlessly, execs, and tears down cleanly.
- Vuln check + approval recorded for any new dep.
nodes.acceptance: A documented API-surface decision and a working headless launch/exec/teardown proof of a Hyper-V-isolated Windows container on Win11 Pro/Server 2022; any new dependency vuln-checked and approved.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Windows mgit-guest agent
nodes.description: A Windows build of mgit-guest (the current one is //go:build linux): in-guest supervisor + clean-env exec + framed IO over HvSocket, transport-only.
nodes.prompt:
Build a Windows mgit-guest agent. The existing internal/guest + cmd/mgit-guest are //go:build linux (vsock, /work virtiofs, tmpfs). Mirror that contract for the Windows guest: supervise command execution inside the WCOW container, clean-env exec (host env never inherited — BaseEnv + per-request env only), framed stdout/stderr/result protocol per FR-17.35 over HvSocket, transport-only with NO signing key (SEC-01, FR-17.6). Reuse the model.ExecRequest/ExecResult types and the framing already defined for the Linux guest where possible; share cross-platform code, keep OS-specific bits behind build tags. Refs: FR-17.6, FR-17.16, FR-17.35, ADR-006.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- cmd/mgit-guest builds for windows/amd64 and runs as the container's entry/supervisor.
- Clean-env exec verified (host environment not leaked into the guest).
- Framed exec protocol over HvSocket matches the Linux guest's wire contract; oversized/malformed requests fail closed.
- No signing material in the guest.
nodes.acceptance: cmd/mgit-guest builds and runs on Windows as the WCOW supervisor; clean-env exec and the framed HvSocket protocol work; transport-only (no signing key); shares the cross-platform exec types with the Linux guest.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: HvSocket control/land transport (AF_HYPERV)
nodes.description: Host↔guest control and land transport over Hyper-V sockets (AF_HYPERV), peer-bound to the VM identity GUID per FR-17.27.
nodes.prompt:
Implement the host-side and guest-side HvSocket (AF_HYPERV) transport for the WCOW backend — the Windows analog of the Linux vsock channel. Register the GuestCommunicationServices service GUID (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Virtualization\GuestCommunicationServices); peer-bind each guest's control/land channel to its VM identity GUID and REJECT messages from any other peer (FR-17.27); invalidate the binding at teardown so a recycled VM GUID cannot inherit a destroyed sandbox's channel. Implement the FR-17.35 framed protocol with enforced ceilings (max object 64 MiB, max objects/land 100000, max payload 4 GiB; canonicalize tree-entry paths, reject traversal/non-canonical). Pure-Go AF_HYPERV via golang.org/x/sys/windows if sufficient, else an approved lib (vuln-check first). Refs: FR-17.27, FR-17.35, ADR-006.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Host and guest exchange framed control/land messages over HvSocket.
- Peer binding to VM-GUID enforced; cross-guest addressing rejected and audited; binding invalidated at teardown.
- Protocol ceilings + path canonicalization enforced (fail closed).
nodes.acceptance: Framed control/land messaging over HvSocket works host↔guest; VM-GUID peer binding rejects cross-guest traffic and is invalidated on teardown; FR-17.35 ceilings and path canonicalization enforced.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: WCOW backend manager (model.SandboxManager)
nodes.description: Implement model.SandboxManager for Windows over the chosen WCOW API; distinct from the Linux/macOS microvm.Manager (container+UVM model). Wire into select.go.
nodes.prompt:
Implement the Windows WCOW backend behind model.SandboxManager (FR-17.15): Launch/List/Exec/Stop/Remove/Resolve over the API chosen in the launch spike. Read-only prepared base image + per-sandbox writable layer (copy-on-write); mount ONLY the bound worktree at the identical absolute path (FR-17.3); sensitive/host-trusted paths mounted read-only (FR-17.14); resource caps + global ceiling (FR-17.26); per-sandbox network policy none/open/allowlist enforced host-side (FR-17.7/8). This is a DISTINCT manager from microvm.Manager (container+utility-VM model, not kernel+rootfs) — do not force-fit the microvm seam. Register the backend in internal/sandboxd/select.go (native Windows backend, not the reduced-isolation fallback). Refs: FR-17.3/7/8/14/15/26, ADR-006.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- SandboxManager implemented for Windows; launch→exec→stop→remove lifecycle works on a real Win11/Server 2022 host.
- Worktree-only mount at identical path; sensitive paths read-only; resource caps + global ceiling enforced; network policy enforced host-side.
- Selectable via select.go as the native Windows backend; missing prerequisites fail closed (no silent downgrade).
nodes.acceptance: A working Windows SandboxManager (launch/exec/stop/remove) on real hardware with worktree-only identical-path mount, RO sensitive paths, resource/network policy enforcement, wired into select.go and failing closed when unavailable.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Prepared Windows base image + SANDBOX-IMAGES register entry
nodes.description: Build/pin a digest-pinned Windows sandbox base image with the dev toolchains; add to images.lock + SANDBOX-IMAGES register with Ed25519 signing.
nodes.prompt:
Produce the Windows sandbox guest image. A prepared, digest-pinned Windows base (Server Core) with the common developer toolchains baked in (Go, Node/npm, Python, MSVC Build Tools or MinGW for C/C++/Rust) — NOT installed at runtime. Pin in images.lock (host-side, FR-17.13/17), add to the SANDBOX-IMAGES.md register (FR-17.31) with version/digest and a known-anomaly review, and sign with the Ed25519 trust root (FR-17.29/38). Provide a version matrix: ltsc2019 (runs on a Win10 22H2 host) and ltsc2022 (Win11/Server 2022 host) — Hyper-V isolation requires host build >= container base build. Document the image build (Dockerfile/script) as a configuration item under FR-17.36 change control. Refs: FR-17.17/29/31/36/38, ADR-006.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A digest-pinned Windows base image with toolchains, reproducibly built and documented.
- images.lock + SANDBOX-IMAGES register entry with digest + known-anomaly review; Ed25519 signature verifies against the trust root.
- ltsc2019 + ltsc2022 variants with the host-build compatibility note.
nodes.acceptance: A reproducible, digest-pinned, signed Windows sandbox base image (ltsc2019 + ltsc2022) registered in images.lock and SANDBOX-IMAGES.md with toolchains baked in and signature verification passing.
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: Provision Windows 11 Pro / Server 2022 runner (Hyper-V + headless containers)
nodes.description: Infra: a Win11 Pro / Server 2022 box (or cloud VM with nested virt) with Hyper-V + Containers + a headless container engine, for building/testing the WCOW backend. Owner: vimal.
nodes.prompt:
Provision the dev/test runner for the WCOW backend. Requirements: Windows 11 Pro OR Windows Server 2022 (for the ltsc2022 base and current Hyper-V isolation), with the Hyper-V and Containers features enabled, nested virtualization if it is itself a VM, a HEADLESS container engine (containerd for Windows, or the chosen HCS path — NOT dependent on Docker Desktop's GUI), SSH access for the agent, and Go. A Win10 22H2 host works only with ltsc2019; Win11/Server 2022 is needed for ltsc2022. Owner: vimal (infra). This blocks the spike (MGIT-12.1) and backend (MGIT-12.4) real-hardware work.

- Win11 Pro / Server 2022 reachable over SSH with Hyper-V + Containers enabled and a headless container engine running.

nodes.title: WCOW integration/e2e, fault-injection & compliance
nodes.description: Real WCOW boot/exec/land/teardown e2e tests, fault-injection categories, and the DO-330 tool-qualification position for the Windows VMM/guest.
nodes.prompt:
End-to-end + compliance for the Windows backend. Real-hardware tests: per-task isolated Windows container boots, execs the toolchain, quarantine-then-land enforces dual-hash (ADR-002) + task-ID binding + host-anchored attestation IDENTICALLY to the Linux backend (FR-17.5/6), residue-free teardown (FR-17.19). nodes.title: Host-side guest vsock dialer (real GuestDialer)
nodes.prompt:
DISCOVERED GAP (2026-06-17, backend-boot track): both microVM backends really boot (vzf real VZVirtualMachine; firecracker real firecracker-go-sdk) and wire a vsock device, but there is NO concrete host-side GuestDialer (microvm.Manager.GuestDialer is the interface; only a test pipeDialer exists). So microvm.Manager.Exec returns ErrSandboxBackendUnavailable and exec/land cannot reach the guest's vsock :1024 listener (cmd/mgit-guest). This blocks the exec/land e2e that 11.6/11.7/11.9 defer to.

Implement the real host-side guest dialer that the daemon injects into microvm.Config.GuestDialer.

PORTABLE CORE (build-tag-free, unit-testable on any OS — DO FIRST): firecracker exposes vsock via a per-VM unix socket with host-initiated connections: the host dials the uds, sends "CONNECT <port>\n", firecracker replies "OK <hostport>\n" on success, then proxies the stream to the guest's vsock port. This handshake is plain I/O over a unix socket and is OS-portable (unix sockets work on macOS+Linux), so the firecracker dialer + handshake is fully unit-testable against a fake firecracker uds server WITHOUT /dev/kvm.

PLATFORM WIRING (needs the hardware, defer): (a) firecracker (linux): the firecracker manager must expose each sandbox's vsock.sock path, and a GuestDialer maps sandboxID -> that path -> the portable Dial; wire PeerBinder.Bind(sandboxID, peer) at launch (SEC-10). (b) vzf (macOS, CGO): VZVirtioSocketDevice.Connect(port) on the running VM returns a VZVirtioSocketConnection — a thin GuestDialer wrapper; needs the VZ virtualization entitlement + a real guest image to test. Real-VM round-trips (exec/land) are e2e (MGIT-11.13), needing the Linux KVM box / entitled Mac.

DEFINITION OF DONE per issue: TDD red->green->refactor; REQUIRED TESTS pass; coverage gates met (other 90%/85%); zero linter/vet/race/vuln findings; /simplify after green; full Verification Checklist. Commit format {type}({scope}): {desc} + 'Refs: MGIT-11.5.x'. NO co-authored-by/agent/Generated-with trailer (information-exposure control).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
The portable firecracker vsock dialer connects to a uds and completes the CONNECT handshake to a guest port, returning a stream; a rejected/garbled handshake and an absent socket fail closed; the dialer satisfies a host-side dial contract the microvm.Manager.GuestDialer can adopt. (Platform manager wiring + real-VM exec/land round-trip are tracked for the e2e step on real hardware.)

REQUIRED TESTS:
  - TestFCVsock_Dial_HandshakeSucceeds
  - TestFCVsock_Dial_HandshakeRejected
  - TestFCVsock_Dial_SocketAbsent
nodes.creator: mcp

nodes.title: vzf (macOS) GuestDialer via VZVirtioSocketDevice.Connect
nodes.prompt: The macOS half of the host-side guest dialer (MGIT-13 delivered firecracker via fcvsock + firecracker.guestDialer). Implement a microvm.GuestDialer for the vzf backend wrapping VZVirtioSocketDevice.Connect(port) on the running VZVirtualMachine, returning the VZVirtioSocketConnection as a net.Conn. nodes.title: Core Model & Storage
nodes.description: Domain types, go-git wrapper, SQLite index, task-commit mapping.
nodes.prompt:
CONTEXT: Core Model & Storage — Waves 2-4

You are building the foundational data layer for mgit. This story covers domain model types, the go-git repository wrapper, and the SQLite task-commit index. Everything in later stories (services, CLI, API, MCP) depends on this being correct.

ARCHITECTURE:
  internal/model/ — Pure types: Commit (16 fields per FR-3), Branch (9 fields per FR-5), TaskID (dot-notation per FR-4), FileDiff (per FR-11), sentinel errors (15+ per model/errors.go). NO I/O in model/.
  internal/store/git/ — go-git wrapper: Repository, CommitStore, TreeStore, BranchStore, DiffStore, WorktreeStore, SyncingStorer. Uses plumbing API (not porcelain) for determinism. Wraps all go-git types — never expose *object.Commit or *git.Repository to callers.
  internal/store/index/ — SQLite: schema.go, migration.go, store.go (dual connection pools: 1 writer, 5 readers), task_commits.go (APPEND-ONLY), branches.go.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:
  - DUAL-HASH MODEL (ADR-002): SHA-1 for go-git object IDs (protocol requirement) + SHA-256 for mgit content_hash (integrity/compliance). Store BOTH in SQLite.
  - APPEND-ONLY (FR-12): task_commits table is INSERT-only. No UPDATE, no DELETE. Ever. Rollbacks create revert commits.
  - CLOCK INJECTION: Never call time.Now() directly. Accept func() time.Time parameter for deterministic testing.
  - FSYNC (NFR-3.3a): SyncingStorer wraps go-git filesystem.Storage with explicit fsync() after every write — crash safety for hospital/airline deployments.
  - SQL SAFETY: Parameterized queries ONLY (?). No fmt.Sprintf in SQL. PRAGMAs: JOURNAL_MODE=WAL, SYNCHRONOUS=FULL, FOREIGN_KEYS=ON, BUSY_TIMEOUT=5000.

DEPENDENCY ORDER: Model types (Wave 2) → go-git wrapper (Wave 3) → SQLite index (Wave 4) → Integration tests (Wave 4).

nodes.title: go-git Repository Wrapper
nodes.description: Implement repository and storage abstractions wrapping go-git library.
nodes.prompt:
SCOPE: Implement the go-git repository wrapper in internal/store/git/. This layer wraps go-git v5 plumbing API to provide deterministic, auditable git operations. NEVER expose go-git types to callers — wrap everything in model types.

STORES TO IMPLEMENT:
  Repository (repository.go) — Init/Open/Close .mgit/ directory. Clock injection for deterministic timestamps.
  CommitStore (commit.go) — CreateCommit (go-git objects + SHA-256 content_hash), GetCommit, ListCommits, DeleteCommit (returns ErrAppendOnlyViolation always).
  TreeStore (tree.go) — BuildTree (from FileDiffs), GetTree, TraverseTree.
  BranchStore (branch.go) — CreateBranch, GetBranch, ListBranches, SwitchBranch, DeleteBranch. Locking support per FR-5.
  DiffStore (diff.go) — FileDiff (between two commits), RangeDiff (over commit range), DiffStats.
  WorktreeStore (worktree.go) — Status, Checkout, Clean.
  SyncingStorer (storer.go) — Wraps go-git filesystem.Storage with fsync() after every write per NFR-3.3a. Critical for crash safety.

KEY RULES:
  - Plumbing API (not porcelain) for determinism and auditability
  - All go-git types wrapped in model types before returning
  - go-git errors converted to mgit domain errors (ErrCommitNotFound etc.)
  - Dual-hash: SHA-1 for go-git object IDs + SHA-256 for mgit content_hash

WAVE GATE: All go-git operations pass against temp repos, no external git binary usage.

nodes.title: SQLite Index Store
nodes.description: Implement SQLite schema, migrations, and task-commit index operations.
nodes.prompt:
SCOPE: Implement the SQLite index store in internal/store/index/. This is the task-commit mapping database — the source of truth for which commits belong to which tasks.

COMPONENTS:
  schema.go — Table definitions: commits, tasks, task_commits (APPEND-ONLY), branches, branch_locks, worktrees.
  migration.go — Schema versioning and incremental migration support.
  store.go — IndexStore: dual connection pools (1 writer, 5 readers), PRAGMA configuration, ReadTx/WriteTx transaction helpers.
  task_commits.go — AddCommitToTask, GetTaskCommits, GetCommitTask (bidirectional), DeleteFromTask (always fails — append-only).
  branches.go — CreateBranch, GetBranch, UpdateBranchHead (forward-only), LockBranch, UnlockBranch.

CRITICAL SQL RULES:

CONTEXT: Service Layer — Waves 5-6

You are building the business logic layer for mgit. Services sit between the store layer (go-git + SQLite) and the presentation layers (CLI, REST API, MCP). ALL business logic lives here — handlers never access stores directly.

ARCHITECTURE:
  internal/service/commit.go — CommitService: CreateCommit (FR-2, FR-3). Accepts task_id, agent_id, session_id, file_diffs, message. Computes tree_hash via TreeStore.BuildTree(). Generates SHA-256 content_hash. Stores in both go-git (CommitStore) and SQLite (IndexStore). Auto-generates messages if not provided.
  internal/service/squash.go — SquashService: SquashTask (FR-7). Retrieves all commits for task, verifies chain integrity, merges FileDiffs into single diff, creates squashed commit atomically. Optional --to-git export. ALL-OR-NOTHING: if any step fails, entire operation rolls back.
  internal/service/rollback.go — RollbackService: RollbackTask (FR-6). Computes inverse diff, creates revert commit (NEVER deletes original). Supports dry_run mode. Append-only enforcement.
  internal/service/branch.go — BranchService: CreateBranch (auto-naming mgit-{TaskID}), SwitchBranch (with locking), MergeBranch, DeleteBranch (refuses unmerged).
  internal/service/diff.go — DiffService: DiffCommits, DiffTask (FR-11).
  internal/service/verify.go — VerifyService: VerifyCommitChain, VerifyTask (FR-12).
  internal/service/audit.go — AuditService: LogOperation, GetAuditTrail (FR-12).
  internal/service/config.go — ConfigService: Load/Get/Set .mgit/config.yaml (FR-13).

DEPENDENCY INJECTION: Every service takes store interfaces + clock func as constructor params. No globals, no singletons.
TESTING: 95% line coverage. Table-driven tests. Integration tests in e2e/ verify services work together.
ERROR HANDLING: Wrap all errors with context using fmt.Errorf("operation: %w", err). Use sentinel errors from model/errors.go.

nodes.title: API & MCP Integration
nodes.description: REST API + MCP server + mtix integration.
nodes.prompt:
CONTEXT: API & MCP Integration — Wave 8

You are building the REST API server, MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, and mtix integration layer. These are the primary interfaces for LLM coding agents to interact with mgit.

ARCHITECTURE:
  internal/api/http/ — REST API on localhost:6860 (configurable). Uses labstack/echo/v4. Token-based auth (NFR-5.11). Endpoints: POST /commits, GET /commits/:hash, GET /tasks/:id/commits, POST /branches, GET /branches, POST /squash, POST /rollback, GET /verify.
  internal/mcp/ — MCP server over stdio (primary) + SSE (secondary). 15 core tools + mgit_docs_generate = 16 total. Uses JSON-RPC 2.0. Logs to file (never stdout — stdout is protocol stream).
  internal/mtix/ — mtix client wrapper. Connects to mtix REST API. Syncs task-commit relationships bidirectionally. Auto-squash trigger on mtix task completion events.

MCP TOOLS (FR-10.2-10.3):
  Core: commit, rollback, squash, status, log (5)
  Advanced: diff, show, branch, verify, export, audit, config (7)
  Worktree: worktree_add, worktree_list, worktree_remove (3)
  Total: 15 core + mgit_docs_generate (Wave 10) = 16

MTIX INTEGRATION (FR-14):
  - Shared task IDs (mgit commit --task MGIT-3.1.2 maps to mtix node MGIT-3.1.2)
  - Event-driven: mtix task done → mgit auto-squash
  - Bidirectional: mgit commit creates mtix comment; mtix status change updates mgit metadata

SECURITY: localhost binding by default. Bearer token auth on all API endpoints. Token generation via mgit token generate (NFR-5.11).

  1000 commits across 100 tasks — measure packfile compression ratio, SQLite index size, total .mgit/ footprint.

nodes.title: Agent Worktrees (Multi-Agent Parallel Development)
nodes.prompt:
CONTEXT: Agent Worktrees (Multi-Agent Parallel Development) — Wave 11

You are building linked worktree support so multiple LLM coding agents can work in parallel on different tasks without interfering with each other. The CLI mirrors standard git worktree semantics so agents trained on git documentation can use them without friction.

DESIGN DECISION (ADR-004): Pluggable WorktreeManager interface. v1 is mgit-managed (filesystem + SQLite registry). v2 will swap in go-git v6 native worktrees when v6 matures (currently too new — 3 weeks old, experimental x/ prefix, known bugs #1406/#1190).

ARCHITECTURE:
  internal/model/worktree.go — WorktreeInfo struct, WorktreeAddOptions, WorktreeManager interface (Add/List/Remove/Prune/Resolve).
  internal/store/index/worktrees.go — SQLite worktrees table: path TEXT PK, name TEXT UNIQUE, branch TEXT, task_id TEXT UNIQUE, agent_id TEXT, created_at TEXT, last_commit_at TEXT.
  internal/worktree/manager.go — v1 WorktreeManager implementation. Creates .mgit/worktrees/<name>/ directories with HEAD, task_binding, agent_id, created_at, locked files.
  internal/service/worktree_service.go — Lifecycle orchestration: auto-create branch on worktree add, task binding enforcement, commit integration (auto-inherit task ID), squash/rollback hooks.
  cmd/mgit/worktree.go — CLI: mgit worktree add/list/remove/prune.
  internal/mcp/ — MCP tools: mgit_worktree_add, mgit_worktree_list, mgit_worktree_remove.

KEY RULES (FR-16):
  1. Every worktree bound to exactly one task (ErrTaskAlreadyBound if duplicate).
  2. No branch sharing between worktrees (ErrBranchInUse).
  3. Commits from worktree auto-inherit bound task ID (ErrTaskMismatch if --task conflicts).
  4. Auto-remove worktree on squash completion (FR-16.7).
  5. Main worktree always present, no implicit task binding (FR-16.12).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Implement v1 WorktreeManager (filesystem-backed)
nodes.prompt:
Create internal/worktree/manager.go implementing WorktreeManager interface per FR-16.4 and FR-16.10. The Add method: (1) validates task_id not already bound (query index.db), (2) validates branch not in use by another worktree, (3) creates .mgit/worktrees/<name>/ directory with HEAD, task_binding, agent_id, created_at files, (4) creates the working directory at the specified path, (5) checks out the task branch into the working directory using go-git worktree operations, (6) registers in index.db via InsertWorktree. The Remove method: deletes .mgit/worktrees/<name>/, removes working directory (if --force), deletes from index.db. The Prune method: scans .mgit/worktrees/ for entries not in index.db or with stale last_commit_at. Create cmd/mgit/worktree.go implementing the worktree subcommand group per FR-16.2. Subcommands: (1) 'mgit worktree add <path> --task <ID> [--branch <name>]' — creates linked worktree bound to task. Default branch name: task/<task_id>. (2) 'mgit worktree list [--porcelain]' — lists all worktrees with path, branch, task, agent, last commit time.
